attributes.action: Increase the funding for mental health support in schools.
attributes.additional_details: We want the funding for mental health in schools to increase so students aren’t as worried to come to school. Students need trained professionals to be able to help them and schools aren’t getting enough money to be able to provide this. Lots of teenagers don’t feel supported enough within schools and this affects everything from their education and their home life and that’s not ok.
attributes.background: We’d like the government to increase the funding for mental health support so students can have trained professionals to help support them so they can feel safer and less scared to go to school.

rejection.details:
We have a number of petitions already open on the subject of mental health support in schools including this one on there being trained counsellors available for each school, which you may wish to sign:
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/751950

Other petitions you may be interested in are:
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/752343
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/752963
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/746351
